Faculty of Engineering and Natural Sciences

About the Faculty

MENU

The primary aim of the Faculty of Engineering and Natural Sciences is to educate engineers who have internalized engineering knowledge, gained the ability to apply it, are familiar with national and international industrial institutions as well as engineering and architectural methods, are firmly committed to professional ethics, and who are innovative and creative individuals prepared for global competition.

Our Mission

Education & Training

To cultivate individuals with ethical values, who      have internalized universal culture and values, and who are both global      citizens and patriots.

To educate individuals with analytical and critical      thinking skills, entrepreneurial and innovative spirit, proficiency in      multiple foreign languages, who have learned to access, use, and share      information, and who possess self-confidence.

To provide a student-centered education that      reflects universal developments and transformations into the curriculum,      employs contemporary educational techniques and methods, and enhances      students’ design and problem-solving skills.

Research & Development (R&D)

To conduct original basic and applied research and      publish the outcomes in reputable international refereed journals.

To transform research results into innovative      designs, development models, and prototypes.

To carry out investigations and research on      scientific and technological trends and play a pioneering role on a global      scale.

Contribution to Society

In line with the concept of the Fourth-Generation      University, to raise public awareness on science and technology trends, to      form clusters, and to guide the industrial sector.

To contribute to the design of original and      competitive products through national and international projects.

To establish R&D companies, obtain patents, and      implement incubation systems.

To support the industrial sector in technology      transfer projects.

Our Vision

To become a globally innovative and pioneering fourth-generation (*) faculty of engineering that can respond to the needs of society and the business world at universal standards.

(*) Fourth-Generation University: An educational model in which universities act not only as teaching and research institutions, but also as centers of innovation, entrepreneurship, and societal impact.

Why Istanbul Okan University Faculty of Engineering and Natural Sciences?

1. Program Diversity & Accreditation

Offers undergraduate programs in Computer      Engineering, Electrical and Electronics Engineering, Civil Engineering,      Mechanical Engineering, Mechatronics Engineering, Automotive Engineering,      Genetics and Bioengineering, Industrial Engineering, Geomatics      Engineering, and Software Engineering.

With MÜDEK (EUR-ACE) accreditation, graduates’      diplomas are recognized in Europe, the USA, Canada, and many other      countries.

2. Modern Laboratories & Research Centers

Facilities include the Transportation Technologies      and Intelligent Automotive Systems Research Center, CAD/CAM Laboratory,      Basic Electronics Laboratory, Power Electronics and Energy Conversion      Laboratory, Heat and Fluid Laboratory, Advanced Electronics and      Communication Laboratory, Civil Engineering Laboratory, Materials      Laboratory, Mechanical Workshop, Automotive Laboratory, Robotics      Laboratory, Genetics and Bioengineering Laboratory, Physics Laboratory,      Industrial Robotics Laboratory, Dynamometer and Electric Vehicle Modeling      Laboratory, and Smart Energy Management Systems Laboratories.

3. OKANOM – Turkey’s First Autonomous Vehicle Project

Istanbul Okan University was the first university      in Turkey to establish a research group on smart and connected vehicles      and has pioneered the country’s first autonomous vehicle,

OKANOM

The UTAS (Transportation Technologies and      Intelligent Automotive Systems Research Center), founded in 2009, has      received numerous grants from the European Union (EU) and TÜBİTAK.

In 2010, Turkey’s first domestically developed      autonomous vehicle, designed at Okan University, was able to navigate a      defined route fully driverless, avoiding obstacles, controlling      acceleration, braking, and steering via computer systems, and safely      stopping when necessary.

By 2025, with the leadership of Istanbul Okan      University, the OPINA (Open Innovation Autonomous Vehicle Development and      Testing Platform) was launched as an open innovation platform to support      SMEs, start-ups, R&D companies, academics, and researchers in      developing, prototyping, testing, and integrating connected and autonomous      vehicle software, serving both Turkey and Europe.

4. Technological Depth & Academic Collaborations

Through UTAS, Istanbul Okan University is the only      Turkish university that is a member of

ERTICO

and

EGVIA.

This membership has given the Faculty a strong      reputation within European science and technology circles, enabling      participation in Horizon 2020 projects and new international consortia.

Founding member of

ITS Turkey (Intelligent      Transportation Systems Association)

, representing Turkish universities      on its board.

Member of

CCAM (Connected, Cooperative, and      Automated Mobility Association)

,

BEPA (Battery Europe)

,

ASAM      (Association for Standardization of Automation and Measuring Systems)

,      and

AUTOSAR

.

5. Advanced Research Areas

Current research focuses on urban traffic-adaptive      autonomous vehicles, unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), automation,      biotechnology, mobility, and ergonomics technologies.

6. Work Experience – O’COOP & PROMES

The O’COOP program provides students with at least      630 hours of workplace training in the 8th semester.

PROMES emphasizes project-oriented education,      requiring students to collaborate with faculty members and industry      representatives to develop projects addressing real-world problems, thus      applying theoretical knowledge in practice.

Erasmus+, ISEP, and other exchange programs provide      opportunities for student mobility with more than 350 partner universities      worldwide.

Through ARPROGED (Research and Project Development      Directorate), students can commercialize their innovative ideas and      establish start-ups.

9. Academic Staff & Continuous Improvement

A strong academic staff supported by YÖKAK and      MÜDEK accreditations ensures continuous improvement with the contribution      of all stakeholders.

10. Campus, Infrastructure & Accessibility

Zero-energy buildings that generate their own      power.

Tuzla Campus offers modern facilities,      laboratories, and research centers, with the advantage of being easily      accessible within the city.

Why Should You Choose the Faculty of Engineering and Natural Sciences at Istanbul Okan University?

Direct involvement in hands-on, project-based      engineering education, Turkey’s first autonomous vehicle project, and TÜBİTAK/EU-funded      projects.

Opportunity to work on solving real-world problems      with cutting-edge technologies.

A strong career foundation with international      collaborations and industrial work experience.

Workplace experience during undergraduate studies      through O’COOP and PROMES.

A supportive ecosystem where innovative ideas can      be transformed into projects, patents, and start-ups.

Highlights of the Faculty of Engineering and Natural Sciences

Departments & Accreditation

: 10+      engineering disciplines, YÖKAK, MÜDEK/EUR-ACE Accreditation

Infrastructure

: Advanced laboratory      facilities

Industrial Projects

: Open Innovation      Autonomous Vehicle Development Platform, TÜBİTAK and EU projects

Advanced Technology

: Cutting-edge      laboratories in unmanned vehicles, biotechnology, sensors, robotics, and      civil engineering

Academic Collaboration

: Numerous academic      and industrial partnerships and memberships in Turkey and Europe

Work & Research Experience

: O’COOP and      PROMES, start-up development, incubation and patent support

International Network

: Erasmus+ & ISEP      exchange programs

Career Pathways

: Opportunities to take part      in industry-linked projects

Campus Life

: Modern, accessible, zero-energy      buildings providing an ideal learning environment
